solution: 
```class Solution:
    def evenOddBit(self, n: int) -> List[int]:
        bin_str = bin(n)[2:]
        even = odd = 0
        for i, bit in enumerate(bin_str):
            if bit == '1':
                if i % 2 == 0:
                    even += 1
                else:
                    odd += 1
        return [even, odd]
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(n))
space complexity: O(log(n))
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution works by first converting the input number to its binary representation as a string. It then iterates over the binary string, counting the number of even and odd indices with value 1. This approach has a high efficiency due to its simplicity and the fact that it only requires a single pass over the binary representation of the input number. The time complexity is O(log(n)) because the number of bits in the binary representation of n is proportional to the logarithm of n. The space complexity is also O(log(n)) due to the storage required for the binary string. The energy implications are low because this solution only requires a single pass over the input data and does not involve any complex operations.
